title: achillea ptarmica subsp. cartilaginea
intro: Achillea ptarmica subsp. cartilaginea, also known as cartilaginous yarrow or strawflower, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Achillea ptarmica subsp. cartilaginea include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and relieving menstrual cramps, among others. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
